evals.prompt: I have a QBR with Nordstrom next week. They're on Gateway Enterprise 3.4 LTS in hybrid mode across AWS. Adoption has been flat — still just the payments team. Help me prep.
evals.expected_output: QBR prep document with business context, value narrative, adoption analysis, recommendations for expanding beyond payments team, and action items

assertions.text: Output follows a structured QBR format with distinct sections, not freeform advice
Flags that 3.4 LTS support ends Aug 2026 and recommends upgrade planning to 3.10 LTS
Specifically addresses the single-team adoption problem with concrete expansion recommendations beyond payments
Has concrete action items with owners or next steps, not just analysis
Frames recommendations in business outcomes not just technical features

evals.prompt: Sony's CP cluster went down last night and their DPs are running on stale config. The eng team is panicking. I need to send an update email to their VP of Engineering and also an internal Slack to my manager.
evals.expected_output: Two communications: a professional customer-facing email and an internal Slack message with business impact

assertions.text: Produces both a customer-facing email AND a separate internal Slack message
Customer email is empathetic and professional — no blame, no excuses, no over-apologizing
Internal Slack message includes business or relationship risk context
Correctly explains that DPs continue operating on cached config during CP outage
Both communications include concrete next steps and update cadence

evals.prompt: Zillow's renewal is in 60 days. I heard through their champion that they've been evaluating Apigee. Usage is decent but they've never adopted Developer Portal or Mesh despite buying Enterprise. What's my play?
evals.expected_output: Renewal strategy with risk assessment, competitive positioning against Apigee, plan to demonstrate unused value, and stakeholder engagement approach

assertions.text: Addresses Apigee evaluation with specific Kong advantages
Identifies Developer Portal and Mesh as unused paid features and proposes adoption plan
Recommends engaging beyond the champion — economic buyer or executive sponsor
Acknowledges 60-day urgency and structures actions with appropriate milestones
Explicitly categorizes the renewal risk level
